expectations.evidence: File exists at outputs/AUDIT-REPORT.md with 100 lines of substantive audit content.

expectations.text: Report contains a Current State table listing all 9 areas (CLAUDE.md, Rules, Skills, Sub-agents, Hooks, Permissions, Settings, MCP, Features)
expectations.evidence: Lines 8-19 contain a markdown table with exactly 9 rows: CLAUDE.md, Rules, Skills, Sub-agents, Hooks, Permissions, Settings, MCP, Features. Each row includes Area, What it is, Status, and Findings columns.

expectations.text: Every finding includes a Source line with a docs.anthropic.com URL
expectations.evidence: All 8 findings have a Source line containing a docs.anthropic.com URL. Examples: 'https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/agents/claude-code/memory' (CLAUDE.md finding), 'https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/agents/claude-code/skills' (skills finding), 'https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/agents/claude-code/permissions' (permissions finding).

expectations.text: Source citations include provenance markers: (via MCP) or (via Playwright CLI)
expectations.evidence: All 8 Source lines include '(via MCP)' as the provenance marker. Example from line 29: '(via MCP) -- "Every project using Claude Code benefits from a CLAUDE.md"'. No '(via Playwright CLI)' markers appear, which is consistent with the audit header stating 'Mode: MCP'.

All 8 findings use one of the three tags: [fix] x2 (No CLAUDE.md, Stale permission rules), [improve] x4 (No rules directory, Sub-agent files, No project-level settings.json, Feature mix), [good] x2 (Well-structured skills, Effective use of supporting files).

expectations.text: Report includes a Priority Actions section with ranked recommendations
expectations.evidence: Lines 84-92 contain a '## Priority Actions' section with a numbered table (1-5). Actions are ranked with [fix] items first (1-2) followed by [improve] items (3-5). Each row includes type, what to do, and why it matters.

expectations.text: Report includes a Next Steps section mentioning Plan mode
expectations.evidence: Lines 94-100 contain a '## Next Steps' section. Line 98 explicitly states: 'Enter Plan mode (Shift+Tab or /plan)'.

claims.claim: The project has no CLAUDE.md at the root, in .claude/, or in any subdirectory
claims.evidence: No CLAUDE.md file appears in the git status or project file listing. The project root contains checkpoint/SKILL.md but no CLAUDE.md.

claims.claim: All 6 skills have proper YAML frontmatter with name and description fields
claims.evidence: Cannot verify from the audit report output alone -- would need to inspect each skill's SKILL.md file directly. The claim is plausible given the project structure but not independently confirmed.

claims.claim: settings.local.json contains 4 permission allow rules referencing /Users/giovannicordova/Documents/02_projects/minimal-framework/
claims.evidence: Cannot verify from the audit report output alone -- would need to read settings.local.json directly. The specificity of the claim (4 rules, exact path) suggests it was read during the audit.

claims.claim: The cc-audit skill has 5 specialist instruction files in agents/
claims.evidence: Cannot verify from output alone. The report names 5 files (instructions-specialist.md, components-specialist.md, automation-specialist.md, integration-specialist.md, adversarial-reviewer.md) which is specific enough to suggest they were actually enumerated.

claims.claim: This project uses 2 of 9 Claude Code features (Skills, partial Sub-agents)
claims.evidence: The Current State table confirms: Skills is [good], Sub-agents is [improve] (partial), and the remaining 7 areas (CLAUDE.md, Rules, Hooks, Permissions, Settings, MCP, Features) are either [fix] or [improve] with findings indicating absence or gaps.

suggestions.assertion: Report contains a Current State table listing all 9 areas
suggestions.reason: This assertion checks that the table exists and lists the areas, but not that the Status column uses valid values or that the Findings counts are accurate. A report with all 9 rows but random statuses would pass.

suggestions.assertion: Every finding includes a Source line with a docs.anthropic.com URL
suggestions.reason: This checks URL presence but not URL validity. A fabricated URL like docs.anthropic.com/nonexistent would pass. Consider verifying that cited URLs correspond to real documentation pages, or at least that the URL path segments are plausible (/memory, /skills, /permissions, etc.).

No assertion checks the quality or accuracy of the findings themselves -- whether the audit correctly identified real issues in the project. A report with plausible-sounding but entirely fabricated findings would pass all 7 expectations. Consider adding an assertion like 'At least one finding accurately describes an actual project configuration issue' verified against the real project state.

No assertion checks the Findings section heading structure (Instructions, Components, Automation, Integration). The skill may define specific sections, and verifying their presence would catch reports that dump all findings in a flat list.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover structural completeness well but lack content verification. The 7 expectations confirm the report has the right sections, tags, and formatting, but a confidently-written report with fabricated findings about a project it never actually inspected would score 7/7. Adding one assertion that cross-checks a specific finding against real project state would significantly increase discriminating power.
